channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on the launch pad during an engine-firing test
item.description: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.
item.content:encoded:
A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.

(Image credit: John Raoux)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Shrey Parikh, 14, wins the Scripps Spelling Bee after a nail-biting 'spell-off'
item.description: This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021
item.content:encoded:
This is the 101st year of the national spelling competition, and the third time it's been decided by a rapid-fire "spell-off" since the practice was introduced in 2021

(Image credit: Allison Robbert)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Black homeowners in St. Louis are still awaiting aid a year after destructive tornado
item.description: Decades of disinvestment in a predominantly Black St. Louis neighborhood left the community especially vulnerable to last year's devastating tornadoes. Now, some worry homeownership rates will drop.
item.content:encoded: Decades of disinvestment in a predominantly Black St. Louis neighborhood left the community especially vulnerable to last year's devastating tornadoes. Now, some worry homeownership rates will drop.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Henderson

item.title: NAACP calls for sports boycott in states where voting rights are threatened
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson about his organization's Out of Bounds campaign that draws a connection between Black student athletes and voting rights.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with NAACP President and CEO Derrick Johnson about his organization's Out of Bounds campaign that draws a connection between Black student athletes and voting rights.
item.dc:creator: Jason Fuller

item.title: White House asked Pentagon to loan money to a company linked to Trump's oldest son
item.description: A ProPublica investigation by Robert Faturechi says White House adviser Peter Navarro asked the Pentagon to approve a loan to a rare-earth magnet company in which Donald Trump Jr. has a stake.
item.content:encoded: A ProPublica investigation by Robert Faturechi says White House adviser Peter Navarro asked the Pentagon to approve a loan to a rare-earth magnet company in which Donald Trump Jr. has a stake.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hodges

item.title: Former senior CIA officer took home gold bars and millions in cash, FBI says
item.description: David Rush, a former senior CIA officer, is accused of theft and making false statements. The trove of gold and cash went missing from his CIA storage space — but it was found in his Virginia home.
item.content:encoded: David Rush, a former senior CIA officer, is accused of theft and making false statements. The trove of gold and cash went missing from his CIA storage space — but it was found in his Virginia home.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: A federal judge in D.C. declines to block Trump's executive order on voting by mail
item.description: A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.

(Image credit: Alex Brandon)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: U.S. street drug deaths keep dropping, but some Western states see deadly overdose surge
item.description: Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose-reversing medication.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers say some structural changes in treating addiction are helping, including wider access to overdose-reversing medication.

(Image credit: John Raby)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: An Ohio pastor-turned-lawmaker backs a Charlie Kirk American Heritage Act for schools
item.description: The bill says it would permit the teaching of the positive impact of Judeo-Christian values in U.S. history. Opponents say it offers a skewed view of history.
item.content:encoded:
The bill says it would permit the teaching of the positive impact of Judeo-Christian values in U.S. history. Opponents say it offers a skewed view of history.

(Image credit: Andrew Spear for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sarah Donaldson

item.title: These swing voters are adding high gas prices into their political calculations
item.description: As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.
item.content:encoded:
As part of NPR's Swing Shift project checking in with swing voters, these Americans are split on who is to blame for high gas prices but they all agree it has an impact on their personal finances.

(Image credit: Illustrations by Tara Anand)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith
